title: Return bytes4 magic value via shl(224, ...) selector encoding for ERC1271/ERC721/ERC1155 callbacks
description: Many EIPs require returning a bytes4 magic value: ERC1271 returns 0x1626ba7e, ERC6551 returns 0x523e3260, ERC721 returns 0x150b7a02, ERC1155 returns 0xf23a6e61/0xbc197c81. shl(224, value) places the 4-byte value in the top of a 32-byte word — the canonical right-padded ABI encoding for bytes4. Branchless encoding: shl(224, mul(magic, success)) returns magic on success, 0 on failure. Or shl(224, or(magic, sub(0, iszero(success)))) returns magic on success, 0xffffffff on failure (ERC1271 convention).
when_to_apply: Any callback returning bytes4 magic value (ERC1271, ERC721 receiver, ERC1155 receiver, ERC6551 isValidSigner)
when_not_to_apply: Functions returning multiple values or non-bytes4 returns
